pages.title: About Laura | Laura Trott MP
pages.meta_description: Laura Trott is the Conservative Member of Parliament for Sevenoaks & Swanley, having first been elected in 2019. She lives in the constituency with her husband and three children
headings: h2: Main navigation
h3: Laura Trott MP for Sevenoaks
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: About Laura | Laura Trott MP Skip to main content Main navigation About Laura Local Plan In Parliament Contact News Residents survey About Laura Tweet Laura Trott is the Conservative Member of Parliament for Sevenoaks & Swanley, having first been elected in 2019. She lives in the constituency with her husband and three children Laura grew up just outside the constituency in Oxted. She went to Oxted School, and won a place to study economics at Oxford University - becoming the first in her family to go to university. After originally training as a management consultant, Laura served in Downing Street from 2011-2016 advising the then Prime Minister, latterly as Head of Strategic Communications. Laura was also as part of the policy team which wrote the 2015 manifesto, focusing on education and family policy. She was made a Member of the Order of the British Empire in recognition of this service. Upon leaving Government she became a partner at a communications firm. Laura served as the Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Pensions) in the Department for Work and Pensions from October 2022 - November 2023, Chief Secretary to the Treasury, HM Treasury from November 2023 - July 2024 and Shadow Chief Secretary to the Treasury from July - November 2024. She currently serves as Shadow Education Secretary. Laura Trott MP for Sevenoaks Footer About RSS Accessibility Cookies Privacy About Laura In Parliament Published and promoted by Laura Trott MP of 6, Wealden Place, Bradbourne Vale Rd, Sevenoaks TN13 3QQ Copyright 2026 Laura Trott MP. pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Laura Trott MP Skip to main content Main navigation About Laura Local Plan In Parliament Contact News Residents survey In Parliament Tweet You can find all my formal contributions to debates in Parliament here. I am working hard both inside and outside the House of Commons chamber to represent the interests of Sevenoaks & Swanley residents - so if there is something you’d like me to raise, please get in touch by emailing [email protected] - please be sure to include your full name and address. I am firmly opposed to this approach, and I have said so in my submission. Our current system of local government is not perfect, but its most important feature is right there in the name - it is local. h2: Shoreham Residents Survey
h3: Laura Trott MP for Sevenoaks
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: Residents survey | Laura Trott MP Skip to main content Main navigation About Laura Local Plan In Parliament Contact News Residents survey Residents survey Tweet Shoreham is one of the most beautiful villages in the whole of Kent. I love spending time here, whether it’s meeting the brilliant teachers and pupils at Shoreham Primary School, walking along the Darent Valley path, visiting Castle Farm or eating and drinking at either the Samuel Palmer, or Mount Vineyard. It is such a precious location and one that I will always stand up for to ensure it remains a great place to live. I know from previous survey’s how much you all care – like me - about protecting our much-loved Green Belt land, improving local rail and bus services and addressing the speeding through the village. But I always want to keep up to date on the things that you are worried about or want changed, so with that in mind I’ve enclosed a short survey so that I can hear from you directly. I look forward to hearing from you. Shoreham Residents Survey Current Your local priorities Leave this field blank 1. What are the three biggest issues facing Shoreham right now? 2. How would you rate the following issues locally? Questions No problem Minor problem Serious problem Roads (potholes) No problem Minor problem Serious problem Public Transport No problem Minor problem Serious problem Access to GP/ dentist No problem Minor problem Serious problem School places/ facilities No problem Minor problem Serious problem Crime and anti-social behaviour No problem Minor problem Serious problem Broadband and mobile signal No problem Minor problem Serious problem Housing, planning and protection green spaces No problem Minor problem Serious problem Other No problem Minor problem Serious problem 3. If one thing could be fixed over the next year locally, what would it be? 4. Is there anything I can do to help you personally? 5. Are there any specific local projects you would like me to champion or visit? 6. Name 7. Postcode 8. Email 9. How long have you lived in Shoreham? Under 2 years 2-5 years 5-10 years 10+ years Other… Enter other… 10. Which age group are you in? Under 18 18-30 31-50 51-70 70+ Other… Enter other… 11. I would like to be kept up-to-date on future campaigns by Laura Trott MP.
